events.label: reasoning
events.text: We need answer JSON only. Need evaluate sequence for AFCI nuisance-trip diagnosis shared neutral. Work in-progress. Need catch sequence violation. Need decide pass/fail/needs_more_info. Scenario: AFCI nuisance-trip diagnosis (shared neutral). Shared neutral is a known cause of AFCI/GFCI nuisance tripping because AFCI/GFCI breakers monitor unbalanced current between hot and neutral; shared neutrals cause false imbalance. In residential, NEC 210.12 requires AFCI protection for branch circuits. Shared neutrals are generally not allowed with AFCI/GFCI breakers unless using two-pole AFCI breaker designed for shared neutral? Actually standard: Multi-wire branch circuits (shared neutral) can be protected by double-pole AFCI/GFCI breaker that monitors both hots and shared neutral. Using single-pole AFCI breakers with shared neutral causes nuisance trips. So diagnosis must identify shared neutral and correct wiring by separating neutrals or installing two-pole AFCI breaker. Sequence violation likely: troubleshooting AFCI without first de-energizing circuit, or not identifying shared neutral before replacing breaker, or resetting/replacing AFCI before correcting shared neutral. Need produce fail decision because non-compliant/dangerous? Shared neutral with single-pole AFCI is a code violation and hazard (nuisance trip, false sense of protection). Work in-progress, component installed-defective. Need actions: de-energize circuit, identify all neutrals, separate shared neutral or install proper two-pole AFCI, verify with megger? Use NEC 210.12, 210.8 maybe GFCI not relevant. Also 300.3(B), 408.41? But references only NEC 210.8, 210.12 per instruction. Could include NEC 210.12 (AFCI) and 210.8 (GFCI) maybe not needed. Need order list maybe steps in correct sequence. The task asks evaluate procedure sequence and state what must happen before work can continue. We can provide order of correct steps. Use step-id generic. Workable maybe empty or activities that can still start. Since in-progress, maybe no new work until corrected. Need fill all fields. percent_complete maybe 40. value 0. sound_source empty? Since not audio. event_time 0, rate 0. confidence 0.95. component_conditions: installed-defective, non-compliant. risk high? Shared neutral with AFCI can cause failure to arc fault protect and nuisance tripping, but not immediate shock unless energized work. Could be high due to fire protection impairment. decision fail. Let's craft JSON. Ensure valid JSON, no extra.

    "AFCI breaker is nuisance tripping due to a shared neutral condition",
    "Shared neutral is incompatible with single-pole AFCI protection and violates proper branch-circuit wiring",
    "AFCI protective function is impaired, reducing fire-safety protection"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "De-energize the affected circuit(s) and verify absence of voltage before continuing",
    "Trace and identify all shared neutral conductors on the AFCI-protected circuit",
    "Separate the shared neutral(s) to dedicated neutrals or replace with a listed two-pole/common-trip AFCI breaker designed for the shared neutral",
    "Confirm neutral-to-ground continuity is correct and no crossed neutrals remain",
    "Test AFCI function per manufacturer instructions and NEC requirements before returning to service"
  ],
  "rationale": "A shared neutral on a single-pole AFCI circuit creates current imbalance that the AFCI interprets as a fault, causing nuisance trips and defeating protection. The wiring must be corrected or a compatible two-pole AFCI breaker installed before the circuit can be considered compliant and safe.",
